title: Plectocephalus rothrockii
taxonomy: Plectocephalus rothrockii is classified within the plant family Compositae, which is now known as Asteraceae. This classification places Plectocephalus rothrockii in the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The species is further classified as a member of the genus Plectocephalus, which belongs to the family Asteraceae. The kingdom it falls under is Plantae, encompassing all plants. At the most basic taxonomic level, it is classified as a species, Plectocephalus rothrockii.

intro: Plectocephalus rothrockii, also known as Rothrock's plectocephalus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Plectocephalus rothrockii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Plectocephalus rothrockii is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
